report_markdown:
## Week of April 20, 2026

You had zero meetings this week. Zero. Your calendar was completely empty for all five days, giving you more uninterrupted time than most people get in a month. You completed 0 out of 6 tasks. Not 2. Not 1. Zero — same three tasks flagged overdue every single morning by Todoist, for five days in a row.

**The avoidance loop is locked in.** Financial tasks went unmoved for the 2nd consecutive week: 0 completed, rescheduled 5 times. Your inbox received 213 emails and you opened all of them — yet TurboTax sent 3 separate "file now or get charged" warnings this week and you haven't responded. That's not busyness. That's the thing sitting in your stomach.

**The high-stakes pile is getting heavier.** USCIS sent you "We have taken an action on your case" on Wednesday — that's the kind of email that warrants a same-day login to check your case status. No follow-up is visible. Your Gmail storage is 99% full (an alert you've seen and not acted on). A recruiter reached out about a role paying up to $280k — it went unanswered. Six actionable threads open, zero replies sent.

**Thursday was your real week.** You applied to Joseph Arnold Lofts, got approved, confirmed June 1 move-in, paid a holding deposit, and signed the digital lease — all in one afternoon. That was 4+ hours of high-stakes, multi-step execution you nailed completely. Every other day the log is flat.

**The one honest thing:** Your capacity for action isn't gone — Thursday proved it — but you can only activate it for things with external deadlines and social pressure. Everything that requires you to self-start a financial or administrative task stays frozen indefinitely, even when you have every hour free.

**Root cause:** The tasks you're avoiding all carry financial or legal stakes that feel too large to start, so free time doesn't help — it just extends the freeze.
root_cause: Free time doesn't cure avoidance when the avoided tasks carry financial or legal stakes — the absence of meetings just reveals the freeze more clearly.
